If id is not defined for a line item it will be considered as a new line item for the option. Multiple line items can be updated in a single request.","summary":"Bulk update estimate option line items","securityDeclarationType":"declared","responses":[{"id":"57a6d71e354b5","code":"200","headers":[],"contents":[{"id":"7dc196e130f8b","mediaType":"application/json","examples":[],"encodings":[],"schema":{"type":"object","properties":{"line_items":{"type":"array","items":{"$ref":"#/components/schemas/LineItemResponse"}}},"$schema":"http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#","x-stoplight":{"id":"3dc4a77482514"}}}],"description":"OK"}],"request":{"body":{"id":"e6a66ccd82e5e","contents":[{"id":"ea1bb179e60f7","mediaType":"application/json","examples":[],"encodings":[],"schema":{"type":"object","properties":{"line_items":{"type":"array","items":{"$ref":"#/components/schemas/BulkLineItemUpdate"}}},"$schema":"http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#","x-stoplight":{"id":"4198cd5a71890"}}}]},"headers":[],"query":[],"cookie":[],"path":[{"id":"46694964d739c","name":"estimate_id","style":"simple","examples":[],"required":true,"schema":{"type":"string","$schema":"http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#","x-stoplight":{"id":"95ece8a236b8a"}},"explicitProperties":["schema","name","in","required"]},{"id":"f7a151278e526","name":"option_id","style":"simple","examples":[],"required":true,"schema":{"type":"string","$schema":"http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#","x-stoplight":{"id":"cc40cc53f51df"}},"explicitProperties":["schema","name","in","required"]}]},"security":[[{"id":"9e3c31a16ac28","key":"Housecall User OAuth Token","extensions":{},"type":"oauth2","flows":{"clientCredentials":{"refreshUrl":"","scopes":{},"tokenUrl":""}}}],[{"id":"81e097ffe689e","key":"Company API Key","extensions":{},"description":"Company API Key Authorization.\n\nAuthorization Header value format [Token {api-key}]","type":"apiKey","name":"Authorization","in":"header"}]],"servers":[{"id":"fffbf054427e2","url":"https://api.housecallpro.com","name":"Housecall v1 API"}]}

snapshot.summary: Housecall version 1 public API
snapshot.data:
openapi: 3.0.0
info:
  title: Housecall v1 API
  version: '1.0'
  description: Housecall version 1 public API
  contact:
    name: Housecall Engineering
    email: apideveloper@housecallpro.com
servers:
  - url: 'https://api.housecallpro.com'
tags:
  - name: Checklists
  - name: Customers
  - name: Employees
  - name: Estimates
  - name: Jobs
  - name: Job Appointments
  - name: Job Invoices
  - name: Job Types
  - name: Leads
  - name: Lead Sources
  - name: Application
  - name: Webhooks
  - name: Materials
  - name: Material Categories
  - name: Price Forms
  - name: Price Book Services
  - name: Service Zones
  - name: Pipeline
  - name: Routes
  - name: Company
  - name: Schedule
  - name: Events
  - name: Tags
  - name: Lead Line Items
  - name: Invoices
paths:
  /application:
    get:
      summary: Get Application
      responses:
        '200':
          description: OK
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/Application'
      operationId: get-applications
      description: Get the application for a company.
      tags:
        - Application
      security:
        - Application API Key: []
        - Housecall User OAuth Token: []
    parameters: []
  /application/enable:
    post:
      summary: Enable Application
      operationId: post-application-enable
      responses:
        '200':
          description: OK
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/Application'
      security:
        - Application API Key: []
        - Housecall User OAuth Token: []
      tags:
        - Application
      description: Enable the application for a company.
  /application/disable:
    post:
      summary: Disable Application
      operationId: post-application-disable
      responses:
        '200':
          description: OK
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/Application'
      security:
        - Application API Key: []
        - Housecall User OAuth Token: []
      tags:
        - Application
      description: Disable the application for a company.
  /checklists:
    get:
      summary: Get Checklists
      tags:
        - Checklists
      responses:
        '200':
          description: OK
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  checklists:
                    type: array
                    items:
                      $ref: '#/components/schemas/Checklist'
      operationId: get-checklists
      description: Get a list of checklists that belong to a job or estimate. Most provide at least one job_uuid or estimate_uuid
      security:
        - Application API Key: []
        - Housecall User OAuth Token: []
      parameters:
        - schema:
            type: number
            default: 1
          in: query
          name: page
          description: Current Page
        - schema:
            type: number
            default: 10
          in: query
          name: per_page
          description: Number of checklists per page
        - schema:
            type: array
            items:
              type: string
          in: query
          name: job_uuids
          description: Id of jobs you want to pull checklists from
          required: true
        - schema:
            type: array
            items:
              type: string
          in: query
          name: estimate_uuids
          description: Id of estimates you want to pull checklists from
          required: true
  /customers:
    get:
      summary: Get Customers
      tags:
        - Customers
      responses:
        '200':
          description: OK
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                allOf:
                  - $ref: '#/components/schemas/PaginatedList'
                  - type: object
                    properties:
                      customers:
                        type: array
                        items:
                          $ref: '#/components/schemas/Customer'
              examples:
                example-1:
                  value:
                    page: 0
                    page_size: 0
                    total_pages: 0
                    total_items: 0
                    customers:
                      - first_name: string
                        last_name: string
                        email: string
                        company: string
                        notifications_enabled: true
                        mobile_number: string
                        home_number: string
                        work_number: string
                        tags:
                          - string
                        lead_source: string
                        addresses:
                          - id: string
                            street: string
                            street_line_2: string
                            city: string
                            state: string
                            zip: string
                            country: string
      operationId: get-v1-customers
      parameters:
        - schema:
            type: string
          in: query
          name: q
          description: 'A query that can be used to search for a customer by name, email, mobile number and address.'
        - schema:
            type: number
            default: 1
          in: query
          name: page
          description: Current Page
        - schema:
            type: number
            default: 10
          in: query
          name: page_size
          description: Number of customers per page
        - schema:
            type: string
            default: created_at
          in: query
          name: sort_by
          description: Customer attribute to sort by
        - schema:
            type: string
            enum:
              - asc
              - desc
            default: desc
          in: query
          name: sort_direction
          description: Ascending or descending
        - schema:
            type: array
            items:
              type: string
          in: query
          name: location_ids
          description: Id of locations you want to pull from. * If X-Company-Id header is set, location_ids will be ignored
        - schema:
            type: array
            enum:
              - attachments
              - do_not_service
          in: query
          name: expand
          description: Array of strings to expand response body
      description: Get a list of customers.
      security:
        - Company API Key: []
        - Application API Key: []
        - Housecall User OAuth Token: []
    parameters: []
    post:
      summary: Create Customer
      operationId: post-customers
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Created
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/Customer'
      description: Create a new Customer.
      requestBody:
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: '#/components/schemas/CustomerCreate'
        description: 'One of first_name, last_name, email, mobile_number, home_number, work_number are required.'
      tags:
        - Customers
      security:
        - Company API Key: []
        - Application API Key: []
        - Housecall User OAuth Token: []
  '/customers/{customer_id}':
    parameters:
      - schema:
          type: string
        name: customer_id
        in: path
        required: true
    get:
      summary: Get Customer
      tags:
        - Customers
      responses:
        '200':
          description: OK
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/Customer'
      operationId: get-customers-customerId
      description: Get the customer via ID.
      security:
        - Company API Key: []
        - Application API Key: []
        - Housecall User OAuth Token: []
      parameters:
        - schema:
            type: array
            enum:
              - attachments
              - do_not_service
          in: query
          name: expand
          description: Array of strings to expand response body
    put:
      summary: Update Customer
      tags:
        - Customers
      operationId: put-customers-customer_id
      responses:
        '200':
          description: OK
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/Customer'
      description: Update customer attributes
      security:
        - Company API Key: []
        - Application API Key: []
        - Housecall User OAuth Token: []
      requestBody:
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: '#/components/schemas/CustomerUpdate'
  '/customers/{customer_id}/addresses':
    parameters:
      - schema:
          type: string
        name: customer_id
        in: path
        required: true
    get:
      summary: Get All of a Customer's Addresses
      tags:
        - Customers
      responses:
        '200':
          description: OK
      operationId: get-customer-customer_id-addresses
      requestBody:
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              allOf:
                - $ref: '#/components/schemas/PaginatedList'
                - type: object
                  properties:
                    addresses:
                      type: array
                      items:
                        $ref: '#/components/schemas/Address'
      description: Get all of a customer's addresses
      parameters:
        - schema:
            type: number
          in: query
          name: page
          description: Paginated page number.
        - schema:
            type: number
          in: query
          description: Number of addresses returned per page.
          name: page_size
        - schema:
            type: string
            enum:
              - created_at
              - updated_at
            default: created_at
          in: query
          name: sort_by
          description: Address attribute to sort by
        - schema:
            type: string
            enum:
              - desc
              - asc
            default: desc
          in: query
          name: sort_direction
          description: Ascending or descending
    post:
      summary: Create an Address on a Customer
      operationId: post-customer-customer_id-addresses
      responses:
        '200':
          description: OK
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/Address'
      requestBody:
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: '#/components/schemas/AddressCreate'
      tags:
        - Customers
      description: Create an address on a customer
  '/customers/{customer_id}/addresses/{address_id}':
    parameters:
      - schema:
          type: string
        name: customer_id
        in: path
        required: true
      - schema:
          type: string
        name: address_id
        in: path
        required: true
    get:
      summary: Get a Customer's Address
      tags:
        - Customers
      responses:
        '200':
          description: OK
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/Address'
      operationId: get-customers-customer_id-addresses-address_id
      description: Get a customer's address via customer ID and address ID.
      security:
        - Company API Key: []
        - Application API Key: []
        - Housecall User OAuth Token: []
  /employees:
    get:
      summary: Get Employees
      responses:
        '200':
          description: OK
          headers: {}
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  page:
                    type: number
                  page_size:
                    type: number
                  total_pages:
                    type: number
                  total_items:
                    type: number
                  employees:
                    type: array
                    items:
                      $ref: '#/components/schemas/Employee'
              examples:
                example-1:
                  value:
                    page: 0
                    page_size: 0
                    total_pages: 0
                    total_items: 0
                    employees:
                      - id: string
                        avatar_url: string
                        color_hex: string
                        first_name: string
                        last_name: string
                        email: string
                        mobile_number: string
                        role: string
                        created_at: string
                        tags:
                          - string
                        permissions:
                          can_add_and_edit_job: true
                          can_be_booked_online: true
                          can_call_and_text_with_customers: true
                          can_chat_with_customers: true
                          can_delete_and_cancel_job: true
                          can_edit_message_on_invoice: true
                          can_see_street_view_data: true
                          can_share_job: true
                          can_take_payment_see_prices: true
                          can_see_customers: true
                          can_see_full_schedule: true
                          can_see_future_jobs: true
                          can_see_marketing_campaigns: true
                          can_see_reporting: true
                          can_edit_settings: true
                          is_point_of_contact: true
                          is_admin: true
      operationId: get-employees
      parameters:
        - schema:
            type: number
            default: 1
          in: query
          name: page
          description: Current page
        - schema:
            type: number
            default: 10
          in: query
          name: page_size
          description: Number of employees per page
        - schema:
            type: string
            default: created_at
          in: query
          name: sort_by
          description: employee attribute to sort_by
        - schema:
            type: string
            default: desc
          in: query
          name: sort_direction
          description: Ascending or descending
        - schema:
            type: array
            items:
              type: string
          in: query
          name: location_ids
          description: Id of locations you want to pull from. * If X-Company-Id header is set, location_ids will be ignored
      description: 'Get all of the active employees in an organiztion. '
      tags:
        - Employees
      security:
        - Company API Key: []
        - Application API Key: []
        - Housecall User OAuth Token: []
  /jobs:
    get:
      summary: Get Jobs
      tags:
        - Jobs
      responses:
        '200':
          description: OK
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                allOf:
                  - $ref: '#/components/schemas/PaginatedList'
                  - type: object
                    properties:
                      jobs:
                        type: array
                        items:
                          $ref: '#/components/schemas/Job'
      operationId: get-jobs
      requestBody:
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              allOf:
                - {}
              type: object
            examples: {}
          application/xml:
            schema:
              type: object
      description: Get a list of jobs
      parameters:
        - schema:
            type: string
          in: query
          name: scheduled_start_min
          description: Filters jobs with a starting time greater than or equal to the date sent.
        - schema:
            type: string
          in: query
          name: scheduled_start_max
          description: Filters jobs with a starting time less than or equal to the date sent.
        - schema:
            type: string
          in: query
          name: scheduled_end_min
          description: |-
            Filters jobs with an end time greater than or equal to the date
             sent.
        - schema:
            type: string
          in: query
          name: scheduled_end_max
          description: Filters jobs with an end time less than or equal to the date sent.
        - schema:
            type: array
          in: query
          name: employee_ids
        - schema:
            type: string
          in: query
          name: customer_id
          description: Filters jobs by a single customer ID (string)
        - schema:
            type: string
            default: '1'
          in: query
          name: page
          description: Paginated page number.
        - schema:
            type: array
            items:
              type: string
              enum:
                - unscheduled
                - scheduled
                - in_progress
                - completed
                - canceled
          in: query
          name: work_status
          description: Work status filter. Returns jobs from all statuses if empty.
        - schema:
            type: number
            default: 10
          in: query
          name: page_size
          description: Number of jobs returned per page.
        - schema:
            type: string
            enum:
              - asc
              - desc
            default: desc
          in: query
          name: sort_direction
        - schema:
            type: array
            items:
              type: string
          in: query
          name: location_ids

                    end_time: '2019-03-27T15:08:08Z'
                    arrival_window_minutes: 60
                    assigned_employees:
                      - id: string

                        color_hex: string
                        first_name: string
                        last_name: string
                        email: string
                        mobile_number: string
                        role: field tech
                        permissions:
                          can_add_and_edit_job: boolean
                          can_be_booked_online: boolean
                          can_call_and_text_with_customers: boolean
                          can_chat_with_customers: boolean
                          can_delete_and_cancel_job: boolean
                          can_edit_message_on_invoice: boolean
                          can_see_street_view_data: boolean
                          can_share_job: boolean
                          can_take_payment_see_prices: boolean
                          can_see_customers: boolean
                          can_see_full_schedule: boolean
                          can_see_future_jobs: boolean
                          can_see_marketing_campaigns: boolean
                          can_see_reporting: boolean
                          can_edit_settings: boolean
                          is_point_of_contact: boolean
                          is_admin: boolean
                properties:
                  start_time:
                    type: string
                    minLength: 1
                  end_time:
                    type: string
                    minLength: 1
                  arrival_window_minutes:
                    type: number
                  assigned_employees:
                    type: array
                    items:
                      $ref: '#/components/schemas/Employee'
                  appointments:
                    type: array
                    items:
                      $ref: '#/components/schemas/Appointment'
                required:
                  - start_time
                  - end_time
                  - arrival_window_minutes
              examples:
                example:
                  value:
                    start_time: '2021-01-10T20:45:00'
                    end_time: '2021-01-10T21:45:00'
                    arrival_window_minutes: 60
                    assigned_employees:
                      - id: string
                        avatar_url: string
                        color_hex: string
                        first_name: string
                        last_name: string
                        email: string
                        mobile_number: string
                        role: field tech
                        permissions:
                          can_add_and_edit_job: boolean
                          can_be_booked_online: boolean
                          can_call_and_text_with_customers: boolean
                          can_chat_with_customers: boolean
                          can_delete_and_cancel_job: boolean
                          can_edit_message_on_invoice: boolean
                          can_see_street_view_data: boolean
                          can_share_job: boolean
                          can_take_payment_see_prices: boolean
                          can_see_customers: boolean
                          can_see_full_schedule: boolean
                          can_see_future_jobs: boolean
                          can_see_marketing_campaigns: boolean
                          can_see_reporting: boolean
                          can_edit_settings: boolean
                          is_point_of_contact: boolean
                          is_admin: boolean
      requestBody:
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              x-examples:
                example-1:
                  start_time: '2021-06-10T19:43:05'
                  end_time: '2021-06-10T20:43:05'
                  arrival_window_in_minutes: 60
                  notify: true
                  notify_pro: true
                  expand:
                    - assigned_employees
                  dispatched_employees:
                    - employee_id: string
              properties:
                start_time:
                  type: string
                  minLength: 1
                  format: date-time
                  example: '2021-01-10T10:45:00'
                  description: Start time of job in iso8601
                end_time:
                  type: string
                  minLength: 1
                  format: date-time
                  example: '2021-01-10T11:45:00'
                  description: End time of job in iso8601
                arrival_window_in_minutes:
                  type: number
                  description: Integer value in minutes of arrival window
                notify:
                  type: boolean
                  description: Notify the customer of the update schedule
                notify_pro:
                  type: boolean
                  description: Notify the employee of the update schedule
                expand:
                  type: array
                  items:
                    type: string
                dispatched_employees:
                  type: array
                  minLength: 0
                  items:
                    $ref: '#/components/schemas/DispatchedEmployee'
              required:
                - start_time
            examples:
              example:
                value:
                  start_time: '2021-01-10T20:45:00'
                  end_time: '2021-01-10T21:45:00'
                  arrival_window_in_minutes: 60
                  notify: true
                  notify_pro: true
                  expand:
                    - assigned_employees
                  dispatched_employees:
                    - employee_id: string
      description: 'Update a jobs schedule. Jobs with multi days feature containing more than 1 appointment can''t be updated through this endpoint, must use appointments endpoints.'
      parameters: []
      security:
        - Application API Key: []
        - Housecall User OAuth Token: []
      tags:
        - Jobs
    delete:

      responses:
        '200':
          description: OK
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                description: ''
                type: object
                x-examples:
                  example-1:
                    object: schedule
                    deleted: true
                properties:
                  object:
                    type: string
                    minLength: 1
                  deleted:
                    type: boolean
                required:
                  - object
                  - deleted
              examples:
                example:
                  value:
                    object: schedule
                    deleted: true

                value:

                  daily_schedule_windows:
                    - day_name: string
                      schedule_windows:
                        - start_time: string
                          end_time: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: OK
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/CompleteScheduleAvailability'
        '400':
          description: Bad request
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  message:

                    description: All errors formatted in a sentence
                  attr1:
                    type: string
                    description: 'Error message for field attr1, where attr1 would be one of the attributes for the schedule availability object'
  /company/schedule_availability/booking_windows:
    get:
      summary: Booking Windows
      tags:
        - Schedule
      operationId: get-company-schedule-booking-windows-availability
      security:
        - Company API Key: []
        - Application API Key: []
        - Housecall User OAuth Token: []
      responses:
        '200':
          description: OK
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/BookingWindows'
              examples:
                example-1:
                  value:
                    booking_windows:
                      - start_time: string
                        end_time: string
                        available: true
                    show_for_days: 0
                    start_date: string
      description: |
        Returns available windows to book a job or estimate for a given time range (default 7 days). Availability is based on the organization's Online Booking settings and actual open time slots per employee.

        **Window duration** (used to compute each availability window):
        - **service_duration** (optional): When provided, must be a positive integer; it is used as the window size and overrides any duration from service_id.
        - **service_id only**: When only service_id is provided, the endpoint uses the service's configured duration (from Online Booking settings) for window size, and still filters by pros assigned to that service.
        - **Neither provided**: Defaults to 30 minutes for backward compatibility.

        Duration resolution order: `service_duration` → service's configured duration (when service_id given) → 30 minutes.
      parameters:
        - schema:
            type: integer
          in: query
          name: show_for_days
          description: An integer value that dictates how many days of the schedule should be shown; defaults to 7
        - schema:
            type: string
          in: query
          name: start_date
          description: A date string formatted like 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS'; defaults to the next day with an available booking window
        - schema:
            type: string
          in: query
          name: service_id
          description: When provided, filters availability by pros assigned to this service and uses the service's configured duration (Online Booking settings) for window size unless service_duration is also provided.
        - schema:
            type: integer
            minimum: 1
          in: query
          name: service_duration
          description: Optional. When provided, must be a positive integer (minutes). Used to compute availability window size and overrides any duration from service_id.
        - schema:
            type: string
          in: query
          name: price_form_id
          description: A string parameter used to look in the specific price form assigned pros booking availability
        - schema:
            type: array
            items:
              type: string
          in: query
          name: employee_ids
          description: An array of strings used to search on provided pros availability

  /invoices:
    get:
      summary: Get Invoices
      tags:
        - Invoices
      responses:
        '200':
          description: OK
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                allOf:
                  - $ref: '#/components/schemas/PaginatedList'
                  - type: object
                    x-stoplight:
                      id: 8vehf17tqgg8r
                    properties:
                      invoices:
                        type: array
                        x-stoplight:
                          id: prgk2z9jx3xcz
                        items:
                          allOf:
                            - $ref: '#/components/schemas/JobInvoice'
                            - type: object
                              x-stoplight:
                                id: 9yl2rmlsiui5y
                              properties:
                                job_id:
                                  type: string
                                  x-stoplight:
                                    id: p2cg7fh3jdpv0
      operationId: get-invoices
      description: 'Get a list of invoices.'
      security:
        - Company API Key: []
        - Application API Key: []
        - Housecall User OAuth Token: []
      parameters:
        - schema:
            type: array
            items:
              type: string
          in: query
          name: location_ids
          description: Id of locations you want to pull from. * If X-Company-Id header is set, location_ids will be ignored
        - schema:
            type: array
            example: open
            enum:
              - open
              - pending_payment
              - paid
              - voided
              - uncollectible
              - canceled
          in: query
          name: status
          description: Filters invoices by status.
        - schema:
            type: array
            example: cus_123
          in: query
          name: customer_uuid
          description: Filters invoices by customer UUIDs.
        - schema:
            type: string
            example: '2024-01-30T00:00:00Z'
          in: query
          name: created_at_min
          description: Filters invoices with a created_at greater than or equal to the input date
        - schema:
            type: string
            example: '2024-01-30T00:00:00Z'
          in: query
          name: created_at_max
          description: Filters invoices with a created_at less than or equal to the input date
        - schema:
            type: string
            example: '2024-01-30T00:00:00Z'
          in: query
          name: due_at_min
          description: Filters invoices with a due_at greater than or equal to the input date
        - schema:
            type: string
            example: '2024-01-30T00:00:00Z'
          in: query
          name: due_at_max
          description: Filters invoices with a due_at less than or equal to the input date
        - schema:
            type: string
            example: '2024-01-30T00:00:00Z'
          in: query
          name: paid_at_min
          description: Filters invoices with a paid_at greater than or equal to the input date
        - schema:
            type: string
            example: '2024-01-30T00:00:00Z'
          in: query
          name: paid_at_max
          description: Filters invoices with a paid_at less than or equal to the input date
        - schema:
            type: number
            example: '10'
          in: query
          name: amount_due_min
          description: Filters invoices with an amount_due greater than or equal to the input amount
        - schema:
            type: number
            example: '10'
          in: query
          name: amount_due_max
          description: Filters invoices with an amount_due less than or equal to the input amount
        - schema:
            type: array
            enum:
              - consumer_financing
              - credit_card
              - ach
              - external
              - mobile_check_deposit
            example: credit_card
          in: query
          name: payment_method
          description: Filters invoices by payment_method.
        - schema:
            type: integer
            default: 1
          in: query
          name: page
          description: Paginated page number.
        - schema:
            type: string
            default: '10'
          in: query
          name: page_size
          description: Number of invoices returned per page.
        - schema:
            type: string
            default: created_at
            enum:
              - amount
              - created_at
              - due_amount
              - due_at
              - invoice_number
              - paid_at
              - sent_at
              - status
              - updated_at
          in: query
          name: sort_by
        - schema:
            type: string
            default: desc
            enum:
              - asc
              - desc
          in: query
          name: sort_direction

  /api/price_book/materials:
    get:
      summary: Get Materials
      description: Get all price book materials under a parent category
      tags:
        - Materials
      security:
        - Company API Key: []
        - Housecall User OAuth Token: []
      operationId: index-materials
      parameters:
        - name: material_category_uuid
          in: query
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
          description: UUID of the material category to fetch materials under
        - schema:
            type: number
            default: 1
          in: query
          name: page
          description: Paginated page number.
        - schema:
            type: number
            default: '10'
          in: query
          name: page_size
          description: Number of materials returned per page.
        - schema:
            type: string
            default: created_at
            enum:
              - name
              - updated_at
              - created_at
          in: query
          description: Material attribute to sort by
          name: sort_by
        - schema:
            type: string
            default: desc
            enum:
              - asc
              - desc
          in: query
          name: sort_direction
          description: Ascending or descending
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Materials fetched
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  object:
                    type: string
                    description: list
                  page:
                    type: integer
                    description: page number of the paginated list
                  page_size:
                    type: integer
                    description: number of items per page
                  total_pages_count:
                    type: integer
                    description: number of pages of the whole document
                  total_count:
                    type: integer
                    description: total records count
                  data:
                    $ref: '#/components/schemas/Material'
                  url:
                    type: string
    post:
      summary: Create Material
      description: Create a new price book material
      tags:
        - Materials
      security:
        - Company API Key: []
        - Housecall User OAuth Token: []
      operationId: create-material
      parameters:
        - name: material_category_uuid
          in: query
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
          description: UUID of the material category to create this material under
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required:
                - material_category_uuid
                - name
                - price
                - cost
              properties:
                material_category_uuid:
                  type: string
                  description: UUID of the material category to create this material under
                name:
                  type: string
                  description: name of the material to be created
                description:
                  type: string
                  description: description of the material to be created
                price:
                  type: integer
                  description: price of the material to be created in cents
                cost:
                  type: integer
                  description: cost of the material to be created in cents
                unit_of_measure:
                  type: string
                  description: unit of measure of the material to be created (i.e. each)
                part_number:
                  type: string
                  description: part number of the material to be created
                flat_rate_enabled:
                  type: boolean
                  default: false
                taxable:
                  type: boolean
                  description: if the material should be taxable on invoice
                  default: false
            examples:
              Create Material With Minimal Information:
                value:
                  material_category_uuid: pbmatcat_123
                  name: New Name
                  cost: 90
                  price: 120
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Material created
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/Material'

  /api/price_book/services:
    get:
      summary: Get Price Book Services
      tags:
        - Price Book Services
      responses:
        '200':
          description: OK
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  services:
                    type: array
                    items:
                      $ref: '#/components/schemas/PricebookServiceList'
      operationId: get-price-book-services
      description: Get a list of price book services.
      security:
        - Application API Key: []
        - Housecall User OAuth Token: []
      parameters:
        - schema:
            type: number
            default: 1
          in: query
          name: page
          description: Current Page
        - schema:
            type: number
            default: 10
          in: query
          name: page_size
          description: Number of items returned per page.
        - schema:
            type: string
            default: created_at
          in: query
          name: sort_by
          description: Price book service attribute to sort by
        - schema:
            type: string
            enum:
              - asc
              - desc
            default: desc
          in: query
          name: sort_direction
          description: Ascending or descending
        - schema:
            type: string
          in: query
          name: q
          description: 'A query that can be used to search for a service by name, description and task_number.'
        - schema:
            type: array
            items:
              type: object
              properties:
                property:
                  type: string
                  enum:
                    - online_booking_enabled
                    - managed_by
                    - flat_rate_enabled
                    - taxable
                    - category
                    - organization_industry
                  description: Filter field name.
                operator:
                  type: string
                  enum:
                    - eq
                  description: Comparison operator.
                value:
                  oneOf:
                    - type: boolean
                    - type: string
                  description: Value to filter by (Boolean for flags, string for text fields).
              required:
                - property
                - operator
                - value
          in: query
          name: filters
          style: deepObject
          explode: true
          description: >
            Deep-object filters sent as `filters[][property]`, `filters[][operator]`,
            and `filters[][value]`. Supported properties: `online_booking_enabled`,
            `managed_by`, `flat_rate_enabled`, `taxable`, `category`, `organization_industry`.
            Only the `eq` operator is supported. Each `filters[][value]` may be a boolean (`true`/`false`)
            or a string, depending on the property.

  /pipeline/statuses:
    get:
      summary: Get Pipeline Statuses
      description: |
        Retrieves a list of pipeline statuses for a given resource type (lead, job, or estimate).
        Returns an empty array for organizations without pipeline enabled.
      tags:
        - Pipeline
      parameters:
        - name: resource_type
          in: query
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
            enum: [lead, job, estimate]
        - name: page
          in: query
          schema:
            type: integer
            default: 1
        - name: page_size
          in: query
          schema:
            type: integer
            default: 10
      responses:
        '200':
          description: OK
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                allOf:
                  - $ref: '#/components/schemas/PaginatedList'
                  - type: object
                    properties:
                      statuses:
                        type: array
                        items:
                          $ref: '#/components/schemas/PipelineStatus'
              examples:
                with_pipeline:
                  summary: Organization with pipeline enabled
                  value:
                    page: 1
                    page_size: 10
                    total_pages: 2
                    total_items: 13
                    statuses:
                      - id: string
                        name: string
                        status_type: string
                without_pipeline:
                  summary: Organization without pipeline enabled
                  value:
                    page: 1
                    page_size: 10
                    total_pages: 0
                    total_items: 0
                    statuses: []
        '401':
          description: Unauthorized
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  message:
                    type: string
    put:
      summary: Update Pipeline Status
      description: |
        Updates pipeline status for lead, job, or estimate.
        The Pipeline domain only allows forward movement to statuses with equal or higher order values.
      tags:
        - Pipeline
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: '#/components/schemas/PipelineStatusUpdate'
      responses:
        '200':
          description: OK
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status_id:
                    type: string
                    description: The UUID of the updated status
                  resource_id:
                    type: string
                    description: The UUID of the resource (lead, job, or estimate)
                  resource_type:
                    type: string
                    enum: [lead, job, estimate]
                  name:
                    type: string
                    description: The name of the pipeline status
        '404':
          description: Not Found
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  error:
                    type: string

    PricebookLaborRate:
      type: object
      properties:
        object:
          type: string
          enum:
            - labor_rate
        uuid:
          type: string
        name:
          type: string
        hourly_cost:
          type: integer
        hourly_price:
          type: integer
        default:
          type: boolean
        services_count:
          type: integer
      required:
        - object
        - uuid
        - name
        - hourly_cost
        - hourly_price
        - default
        - services_count
    PricebookServiceAssignedProList:
      type: object
      properties:
        object:
          type: string
          enum:
            - list
        data:
          type: array
          items:
            $ref: '#/components/schemas/PricebookServiceAssignedPro'
      required:
        - object
        - data
    PricebookServiceAssignedPro:
      type: object
      properties:
        object:
          type: string
          enum:
            - assigned_pro
        uuid:
          type: string
        full_name:
          type: string
      required:
        - object
        - uuid
        - full_name
    PricebookServiceCategory:
      type: object
      properties:
        id:
          type: integer
        name:
          type: string
      required:
        - id
        - name
    PricebookServiceIndustry:
      type: object
      properties:
        id:
          type: integer
        name:
          type: string
      required:
        - id
        - name
    ServiceZoneResponse:
      title: ServiceZoneResponse
      type: object
      properties:
        id:
          type: string
          format: uuid
        name:
          type: string
        coverage_type:
          type: string
        trip_charge:
          type: integer
        fee_name:
          type: string
        zip_codes:
          type: array
          items:
            type: string
        cities:
          type: array
          items:
            $ref: '#/components/schemas/ServiceZoneCity'
        service_pros:
          type: array
          items:
            $ref: '#/components/schemas/ServiceZoneServicePro'
      required:
        - id
        - name
        - coverage_type
        - zip_codes
        - cities
        - service_pros
    ServiceZoneCity:
      title: ServiceZoneCity
      type: object
      properties:
        city:
          type: string
        state:
          type: string
        country:
          type: string
      required:
        - city
        - state
        - country
    ServiceZoneServicePro:
      title: ServiceZoneServicePro
      type: object
      properties:
        id:
          type: string
        first_name:
          type: string
        last_name:
          type: string
        email:
          type: string
        full_name:
          type: string
      required:
        - id
        - first_name
        - last_name
        - email
        - full_name
    Route:
      title: Route
      type: object
      description: A route groups employees and their scheduled job appointments, events, and estimates for a given date.
      properties:
        id:
          type: string
          description: The unique identifier of the route.
        name:
          type: string
          description: The display name of the route.
        color_hex:
          type: string
          description: The hex color code used to visually distinguish this route (e.g. "#FF5733").
        date:
          type: string
          description: The date this route is scheduled for, formatted as YYYY-MM-DD.
        employee_ids:
          type: array
          description: IDs of employees assigned to this route.
          items:
            type: string
        job_appointments:
          type: array
          description: Job appointments included in this route.
          items:
            type: object
        event_ids:
          type: array
          description: IDs of events included in this route.
          items:
            type: string
        estimate_ids:
          type: array
          description: IDs of estimates included in this route.
          items:
            type: string
    PipelineStatus:
      title: PipelineStatus
      type: object
      description: A pipeline status for lead, job, or estimate.
      properties:
        id:
          type: string
          description: The unique identifier of the pipeline status (e.g. kcs_...)
        name:
          type: string
          description: The display name of the status
        status_type:
          type: string
      required:
        - id
        - name
        - status_type
    PipelineStatusUpdate:
      title: PipelineStatusUpdate
      type: object
      description: |
        Request body for updating pipeline status.

        Note: Only forward movement is allowed. The target status must have an order value greater than or equal to the current status.
      properties:
        resource_type:
          type: string
          enum:
            - lead
            - job
            - estimate
        resource_id:
          type: string
          description: the UUID of the resource
        status_id:
          type: string
          description: the UUID of the pipeline status
      required:
        - resource_type
        - resource_id
        - status_id
  securitySchemes:
    Company API Key:
      name: Authorization
      type: apiKey
      in: header
      description: |-
        Company API Key Authorization.

        Authorization Header value format [Token {api-key}]
    Application API Key:
      name: Authorization
      type: apiKey
      in: header
      description: |-
        Application API Key Authorization.

        Authorization Header value format [Token {api-key}]
    Housecall User OAuth Token:
      type: oauth2
      description: ''
      flows:
        clientCredentials:
          tokenUrl: ''
          refreshUrl: ''
          scopes: {}

snapshot.summary: Welcome to the Housecall Pro Public API changelog, your go-to resource for staying up-to-date on all the changes and updates to our API. Here, we'll provide a comprehensive list of all additions, updates, and modifications to our API in chronologi...
snapshot.data:
# Changelog

## Track changes on Housecall Pro Public API

Welcome to the Housecall Pro Public API changelog, your go-to resource for staying up-to-date on all the changes and updates to our API. Here, we'll provide a comprehensive list of all additions, updates, and modifications to our API in chronological order. Whether you're a developer integrating with our API, or simply interested in staying informed about our latest updates, this changelog will help you stay up-to-date with all the changes to the Housecall Pro Public API.

 [Subscribe to this changelog via RSS.](https://zapier.com/engine/rss/14274546/housecallpro-api-changelog)

 #### Mar, 03, 2026
 - Add `canceled_at` and `deleted_at` to Job protocol and job webhooks. `canceled_at` is set when a job is user canceled (ISO8601 UTC); `deleted_at` when pro canceled/deleted. Both are `null` otherwise.
 - Add `lost_at` to Lead protocol and lead webhooks. When the lead is marked lost, `lost_at` is set (ISO8601 UTC); otherwise `null`.

 #### Feb, 18, 2026
 - Add support for listing pipeline statuses (GET /pipeline/statuses)
 - **Booking Availability (GET /company/schedule_availability/booking_windows):** Availability windows now use the correct service duration instead of always defaulting to 30 minutes. New optional query parameter `service_duration` (positive integer, minutes) lets you set the window size explicitly and overrides any duration from `service_id`. When only `service_id` is provided, the endpoint uses the service's configured duration from Online Booking settings. When neither is provided, the 30-minute default is kept for backward compatibility. Duration resolution order: `service_duration` → service's configured duration (when `service_id` given) → 30 minutes.

 #### Feb, 03, 2026
 - Add support for updating pipeline status for leads, jobs, and estimates. 

 #### Jan, 27, 2026
 - Add support for bulk updating estimate option line items (PUT /estimates/{estimate_id}/options/{option_id}/line_items/bulk_update)

 #### Jan, 22, 2026
 - Add support for Service Zones List endpoint

 #### Jan, 20, 2026
 - Exposing created_at on employee protocol

 #### Jan 5,2026
 - Add `job_id`  to invoice webhooks
 - Add `recurrence_number` and `recurrence_rule` to job protocol
 - Add support for listing lead line items

 #### Dec, 18, 2025
 - Add support for listing estimate line items

 #### Dec, 5, 2025
 - Add support for Pricebook services

#### Dec, 1, 2025
- Add support for creating jobs with anytime schedule.

#### Nov, 24, 2025
- Add support for Invoices webhook events.
- Add `id` field to invoice-related entities (InvoiceItem, InvoiceTax, InvoiceDiscount, InvoicePayment) returned in GET /invoices endpoint.

#### Nov, 20, 2025
- Add support for sorting invoices list endpoint.

#### Nov, 13, 2025
- Add `event_created_at` to all webhook objects.

#### Nov, 11, 2025
- Add support for approving estimate options (POST /estimates/options/approve).
- Add support for declining estimate options (POST /estimates/options/decline).

#### Nov, 10, 2025
- Added `pipeline_status` to the Lead response object.

#### Oct, 27, 2025
- Add support for converting a Lead into a job or estimate. (POST /leads/{lead_id}/convert)

#### Oct, 23, 2025
- Added support for the X-Company-Id header, allowing API requests to target a specific location within a multi-location account.

#### Oct, 20, 2025
- Updated line item `kind` enum values to reflect actual API behavior: removed `tax` (not accepted) and ensured `percent discount` is documented across all line item schemas


#### Oct, 1, 2025
- Add support for locking Job by ID (POST /jobs/:job_id/lock)
- Add support for locking multiple "completed" or "scheduled" Jobs by a given time range (POST /jobs/lock)

#### Sep, 24, 2025
- Add support for retrieving individual invoices by UUID (GET /api/invoices/:uuid)
- Add support for previewing invoices as HTML (GET /api/invoices/:uuid/preview)

#### Sep 23, 2025
- Added `subtotal` to Invoice object
- Updated bulk line items update endpoint to support `line_item.id` for consistency across endpoints (`line_item.uuid` is still supported)

#### Oct, 17, 2024
- Add support for Pricebook materials

#### May, 28, 2024
- Add support for exposing jobs invoices list

#### April, 24, 2024
- **[BREAKING CHANGE]** POST attachment endpoints now only accept binary files from local machine

#### Dec, 6th, 2023
- **[BREAKING CHANGE]** Updates to response objects and http codes for Job Attachments and Estimate Attachments endpoints

#### Nov, 21, 2023
- Fix OAuth tokens expiration time validation. If access token is expired you'll get a 401 error. Make sure to refresh it following our [OAuth docs](https://docs.housecallpro.com/docs/housecall-public-api/b87d37ae48a0d-authentication)

#### Jul, 28, 2023
- Add support for exposing Multi day jobs appointments
- Update lead.deleted webhook protocol

#### Jun, 6, 2023

- Add support for expanding Attachments for Get Job, Jobs, Estimate, Estimates, Customer and customers endpoints
- Add support for Estimate Schedule Update endpoint

#### May 15, 2023

- Add support for Leads List filter by location_ids

#### May 11, 2023

- Add support for Job Fields in Job creation and add it to Job response object.
- Add support for Estimate Fields in Estimate creation and add it to Estimate response object.

#### April 25, 2023

- Add support for Job Fields-Job Types List, Create and Update endpoints.

#### April 20, 2023

- Add support for Estimates attachments creation

#### March 27, 2023

- Add support for Lead Sources List, Create and Update endpoints.

#### March 23, 2023

- Add support for Customers Update endpoint.
- Add support for Estimates Create and Update notes endpoints.
- Add support for Jobs Create and update notes endpoints.

#### March 7, 2023

- Add support for Leads List endpoint.
- Add support for Leads webhook events.

#### February 16, 2023

- Add support for Leads Create and Show endpoints.

Housecall Pro uses both API keys and OAuth 2.0 as a means of authenticating with our API.
snapshot.data:
# Authentication

Housecall Pro uses both API keys and OAuth 2.0 as a means of authenticating with our API.
- If you are a Pro or represent a Pro using Housecall Pro, you will use an **API Key** to build custom integrations. Learn more about [generating your API Keys here.](https://help.housecallpro.com/en/articles/8505035-api-overview#h_208764ce84)
- If you are a verified [Integration Partner](https://docs.housecallpro.com/#-becoming-an-integration-partner), you will use **OAuth 2.0** to authenticate users. 
- If you are a [Partner Jobs Integration Partner](https://docs.housecallpro.com/#%F0%9F%A7%B0--partner-jobs-api) generating jobs or leads, the instructions found at [this link](https://docs.housecallpro.com/docs/partner-jobs/b87d37ae48a0d-authentication) will help guide you through the authentication process


Housecall Pro expects the OAuth 2.0 access token or API key to be included in all authenticated API requests to the server in the Authorization header. This looks like the following:

```
#API key

Authorization: Token 0046421dc0ba47ec87bef0c089415380
```
OR 
```
#OAuth 2.0

Authorization: Bearer d7f65570a4714ae944fbe58acc05ffb20c29534170ac483a3683b91f8dfe9d43
```

> It is important to note that the headers must follow these exact formats, including “Token” or “Bearer” at the beginning of your header. If you receive an authentication error, please send your cURL command to apideveloper@housecallpro.com so we may investigate.


## 🔐 Setting Up OAuth 2.0 Authorization with Housecall Pro (For Official Integration Partners Only)
- To integrate your application with Housecall Pro using OAuth 2.0, follow the steps below to complete the **Authorization Code Flow**.
> **Note**: OAuth 2.0 is available exclusively for *official integration partners*. All other developers should use **API key authentication**, as outlined above.

---


### 🎯 OAuth 2.0 Background

OAuth 2.0 is an industry-standard authorization framework that allows third-party applications to access user data without exposing user credentials. For Housecall Pro integrations, OAuth 2.0 provides:

- **Secure Access**: Users never share their Housecall Pro passwords with your application
- **Controlled Permissions**: Users can grant specific access levels through scopes
- **Revokable Access**: Users can revoke access at any time from their Housecall Pro account
- **Token Expiration**: Access tokens expire automatically for enhanced security

We use the **Authorization Code Flow**, which is the most secure OAuth 2.0 flow for server-side applications. This flow involves redirecting users to Housecall Pro for authentication, then exchanging an authorization code for access tokens.

**User Experience**: Your users will be redirected to Housecall Pro to log in, see what permissions your app is requesting, and then return to your application with authorization granted.

---

> **🔑 OAuth Endpoints:**
> 
> - **Authorization**: `https://pro.housecallpro.com/oauth/authorize`  
>   *Used for user login and authorization*
> - **Token Exchange**: `https://api.housecallpro.com/oauth/token`  
>   *Used for getting and refreshing access tokens*

### 📩 1. Request OAuth App Credentials

In order to get your application set up as an OAuth client, email [apideveloper@housecallpro.com](mailto:apideveloper@housecallpro.com) with:

- Your **application name** and **purpose**
- Your `redirect_uri` (also called `CALLBACK_URL`) — where Housecall Pro will redirect users after authorization

Once approved, you will receive:

- `CLIENT_ID`
- `CLIENT_SECRET`
- `REQUESTED_SCOPES` (if applicable)

Once you have both of these credentials, you may begin the AUTHORIZATION_CODE OAuth flow.

---

### 🚦 2. Initiate the OAuth Flow

Redirect users to the authorization URL:

```
curl -X GET https://pro.housecallpro.com/oauth/authorize?response_type=code&client_id=CLIENT_ID&redirect_uri=CALLBACK_URL&scope=REQUESTED_SCOPES
```
Replace:

- `CLIENT_ID` with your provided client ID
- `CALLBACK_URL` with your registered redirect URI (must match exactly)
- `REQUESTED_SCOPES` (optional)

**Example:**

```bash
https://pro.housecallpro.com/oauth/authorize?response_type=code&client_id=abc123xyz&redirect_uri=https://YOUR_WEBSITE_URL/oauth/callback&scope=public
```

This step must be performed using `curl` or in a **browser** (not Postman), as it requires user login and redirect handling.

🔎 Note:
The OAuth flow begins at https://pro.housecallpro.com/oauth/authorize (for user interaction), but all token exchanges happen via https://api.housecallpro.com/oauth/token.

---

### 👤 3: User Logs In and Authorizes Access

The user will:

1. Log into their Housecall Pro account (if not already logged in)
2. Be prompted to authorize your application

* After clicking the Authorization code link, the user will be prompted to log in to Housecall Pro in order to authenticate their identity, unless they are already logged in. Once authenticated, the user will be prompted to authorize the application to access their Housecall account.

* Once the user authorizes the application, the server will redirect the user's agent to the provided CALLBACK_URL with an AUTHORIZATION_CODE.

```
https://YOUR_WEBSITE_URL/auth/housecall/callback?code=AUTHORIZATION_CODE
```

* The OAuth client will then request an access token from the API by passing the AUTHORIZATION_CODE along with the CLIENT_SECRET.

---

### 🔄 4: Exchange Authorization Code for Access Token

Use the authorization code to request an access token.

**Request:**

```bash
$ curl -X POST \
  https://api.housecallpro.com/oauth/token \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{
      "client_id": "CLIENT_ID",
      "client_secret": "CLIENT_SECRET",
      "grant_type": "authorization_code",
      "code": "AUTHORIZATION_CODE",
      "redirect_uri": "CALLBACK_URL"
  }'
```

**Example Response:**

```json
{
  "access_token": "ACCESS_TOKEN",
  "token_type": "Bearer",
  "expires_in": 2592000,
  "refresh_token": "REFRESH_TOKEN",
  "scope": "public",
  "created_at": "1750104252"
}
```
> `expires_in` represents the token lifetime in seconds.

**Invalid/expired/revoked token response:**

```json
{
  "error": "invalid_grant",
  "error_description": "The provided authorization grant is invalid, expired, revoked, does not match the redirection URI used in the authorization request, or was issued to another client."
}
```


* If the authorization is successful, the API will respond with an OAuth 2.0 access token & an OAuth 2.0 refresh token to the application. You are now authorized! You may now use the token to access the Pro's account through the API provided by Housecall Pro until the token expires or access is revoked.

* Our OAuth 2.0 access tokens have finite lifetimes; use the refresh token provided in the original token grant to request a new access token when the access token expires. Refresh tokens may be requested without user interaction by the same process as requesting an access token, changing the grant_type from authorization_code to refresh_token.


### ✅ 5: Call Housecall Pro APIs

Use the `access_token` to authenticate API requests:

```http
GET /api/example_endpoint
Authorization: Bearer ACCESS_TOKEN
```

Make sure the requested resource is within the scope the user granted.

---

### 🔁 Refreshing Expired Access Tokens

Once your `access_token` expires, use the `refresh_token` to obtain a new one.

**Request:**

```
$ curl -X POST \
  https://api.housecallpro.com/oauth/token \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{
      "client_id": "CLIENT_ID",
      "client_secret": "CLIENT_SECRET",
      "grant_type": "refresh_token",
      "refresh_token": "the_refresh_token_value_goes_here",
      "redirect_uri": "CALLBACK_URL"
  }'
```

**Example Response:**

```json
{
    "access_token": "ACCESS_TOKEN",
    "token_type": "Bearer",
    "expires_in": 2629745,
    "refresh_token": "REFRESH_TOKEN",
    "scope": "public",
    "created_at": 1752092974
}
```
> `expires_in` represents the token lifetime in seconds.

**Revoked Token Response:**

```json
{
  "error": "invalid_grant",
  "error_description": "The provided authorization grant is invalid, expired, revoked, does not match the redirection URI used in the authorization request, or was issued to another client."
}
```

### 🧪 Testing Notes

- **Do not test `/oauth/authorize` using Postman.** It requires a browser to handle login and redirect interactions.
- For local development, ensure:
  - Your app is running at the `CALLBACK_URL`
  - The redirect URI matches exactly what was registered with Housecall Pro
  - You are using the correct `client_id`, `client_secret`, and `scopes`

---

### 📘 Need Help?

If you encounter issues or have questions, reach out to:  
📧 [apideveloper@housecallpro.com](mailto:apideveloper@housecallpro.com)

---
stoplight-id: p5zsg5n9sbiij
---

# Pricebook API

## Overview
The Price Book Public API provides our third-party partners with the ability to create a catalog of their materials directly in a Pro's account. By leveraging this API, partners can efficiently manage material data and ensure accurate and up-to-date information and organization-specific pricing for our users.

## Setup Requirements
All requests made through the Pricebook Public API require an API key. The API key used is specific to a single organization, allowing for organization-specific material pricing.

## Included API Endpoints
### Materials
* Index Materials 
* Create Material 
* Update Material 
* Delete Material

### Material Categories 
* Index Material Categories 
* Create Material Category
* Update Material Category
* Delete Material Category

### Price Forms
* Index Price Forms
* Create Price Form
* Show Price Form
* Update Price Form
* Delete Price Form

snapshot.summary:
Returns available windows to book a job or estimate for a given time range (default 7 days). Availability is based on the organization's Online Booking settings and actual open time slots per employee.

**Window duration** (used to compute each av...
snapshot.data:
{"id":"c80920efe7ef1","method":"get","path":"/company/schedule_availability/booking_windows","tags":[{"id":"af95774df15ee","name":"Schedule"}],"extensions":{},"iid":"get-company-schedule-booking-windows-availability","description":"Returns available windows to book a job or estimate for a given time range (default 7 days). Availability is based on the organization's Online Booking settings and actual open time slots per employee.

**Window duration** (used to compute each availability window):
- **service_duration** (optional): When provided, must be a positive integer; it is used as the window size and overrides any duration from service_id.
- **service_id only**: When only service_id is provided, the endpoint uses the service's configured duration (from Online Booking settings) for window size, and still filters by pros assigned to that service.
- **Neither provided**: Defaults to 30 minutes for backward compatibility.

Duration resolution order: `service_duration` → service's configured duration (when service_id given) → 30 minutes.
","summary":"Booking Windows","securityDeclarationType":"declared","responses":[{"id":"422778c34a2a5","code":"200","headers":[],"contents":[{"id":"0f5814b235b88","mediaType":"application/json","examples":[{"id":"993a61eb6d27e","key":"example-1","value":{"booking_windows":[{"start_time":"string","end_time":"string","available":true}],"show_for_days":0,"start_date":"string"}}],"encodings":[],"schema":{"title":"BookingWindows","type":"object","properties":{"booking_windows":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"object","properties":{"start_time":{"type":"string","description":"A timestamp of the start time of a booking window (UTC)"},"end_time":{"type":"string","description":"A timestamp of the finish time of a booking window (UTC)"},"available":{"type":"boolean","description":"The value representing if an organization is available during the booking window"}}}},"show_for_days":{"type":"integer"},"start_date":{"type":"string"}},"x-examples":{"example-1":{"booking_windows":[{"start_time":"string","end_time":"string","available":true}],"show_for_days":0,"start_date":"string"}},"$schema":"http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#","x-stoplight":{"id":"7246bf6a74a35"}}}],"description":"OK"}],"request":{"headers":[],"query":[{"id":"28deeef8dbfc4","name":"show_for_days","style":"form","examples":[],"description":"An integer value that dictates how many days of the schedule should be shown; snapshot.summary: Get a list of invoices.
Housecall Pro Admins with multiple locations can pull data across all of their locations using a single API key. Data is available across all endpoints.
snapshot.data:
# Multi-location Enabled APIs

## Overview

Housecall Pro Admins with multiple locations can pull data across all of their locations using a single API key. Data is available across all endpoints.
    
## Multi-location API Setup Requirements 

In order to pull data across locations, you must 
- (1) be an admin user of a Housecall Pro account that has multiple locations associated with it.
- (2) have [authentication setup](https://docs.housecallpro.com/docs/housecall-public-api/b87d37ae48a0d-authentication) as an owner or admin of a multi location account.

**To query or manage data across multiple locations using the API, follow these steps:**

### Step 1: Use our GET company endpoint to get your location IDs

- Endpoint: GET https://api.housecallpro.com/company
- You will receive all of your locations information in the `locations` object.
- Record all location IDs, as you will use them in later steps.
- Additional information about each location is also included for your convenience.

### Step 2: Use the X-Company-Id header to specify location context

- When making requests to any supported endpoint, include the X-Company-Id header with the desired location ID.
- **Example:**
  ```http
  X-Company-Id: ce348586-c67a-4d5b-812f-9480514f84f2
- The header determines which locations’s data the request applies to.
- You are all set! You can now access and manage data for specific locations using the same API key.

## Access and Authorization Notes

- An API key issued to a location grants access to data belonging to that location and any locations beneath it in the hierarchy.
- A parent location’s API key can access data for its sub-locations.
- Similarly, a sub-location’s API key can access data for its own nested locations, if applicable.
- Access is always restricted to the location and its descendants — API keys cannot access data for unrelated or higher-level locations.

### Hierarchy Example
```
Main Location (API key owner)
│
├── Sub-location A
│   ├── Nested Location A1
│   └── Nested Location A2
│
└── Sub-location B 
  ├── Nested Location B1
  └── Nested Location B2
```

If the API key belongs to Main Location, it can access data for Sub-location A, Sub-location B, and all their nested locations.

If the API key belongs to Sub-location A, it can access A1 and A2, but not B or B1.

If the API key belongs to A1, it can only access its own data — it cannot access data for A2, B, or the Main Location.


## OAuth 2.0 and Accessible Organizations

When using OAuth 2.0 authentication (available for official integration partners), access to locations may be further restricted based on the authenticated user's **accessible organizations** permissions.

Unlike API keys which grant organization-level access, OAuth tokens provide user-level access control. The authenticated user may only have permission to access a subset of locations within the organization hierarchy.

### Restricted Access Example
```
Main Location (OAuth user's organization)
│
├── Sub-location A (User HAS access)
│   ├── Nested Location A1 (User HAS access)
│   └── Nested Location A2 (User has NO access)
│
└── Sub-location B (User has NO access)
    ├── Nested Location B1 (User has NO access)
    └── Nested Location B2 (User has NO access)
```

In this scenario:
- `/company` endpoint returns only Sub-location A and Nested Location A1 in the `locations` array
- API requests with `location_ids: [A, A1]` succeed and return data
- API requests with `location_ids: [A2]` or `location_ids: [B]` return `403 Forbidden`
- API requests with `location_ids: [A, B]` return `403 Forbidden` (contains unauthorized location)
- Franchise-wide queries (without `location_ids`) automatically filter to accessible locations only

## Backward Compatibility Note
The previously supported `location_ids` parameter is still available for backward compatibility.
 However, when both `location_ids` and the X-Company-Id header are used together the `location_ids` parameter will be ignored.


We recommend migrating to the X-Company-Id header approach for clearer and more consistent behavior across multi-location integrations
